meta.setting: The Gulf of Everroth region. Two neighboring nations — Fastingbreak (declining hereditary monarchy) and Elkenquay (thriving republic) — are being manipulated toward war by a covert nationalist organization called the Concord, operating from within Fastingbreak. A third actor, the wizard Dreknar, is using the growing conflict to pursue his own agenda: raising Zarathrax, Lord of the Seventh Circle of Hell, into the material plane.
meta.theme: Good people making reasonable decisions with incomplete information inside systems that are manipulating them. The party are not villains. They are people trying to help who slowly realize that history is shaped not only by evil acts, but by fear, loyalty, nationalism, misinformation, and the desire to protect the people closest to you.
meta.tone: Mature, morally complex. The Concord should feel like a network of capable patriots who slowly convinced themselves they had to manipulate events to save their country — not a secret evil council. Most members genuinely believe they are protecting their homeland. The party's trust in them makes the eventual revelation more painful.
meta.dramatic_irony: The party believes Elkenquay is destabilizing the region. The Concord is manufacturing that instability. The war the party will help start is not inevitable — it is being engineered around them, piece by piece, using their competence and good intentions as tools.

campaign_structure.prologue: Hollowgate → Moonlit Mirage Carnival → Dunmore. Three escalating monstrosity encounters (a subplot — evidence of something deeper). Party recovers the Book of the Damned without knowing what it is. The Concord saves them after the Dunmore monstrosity. Party is recruited by Cael Darro.
campaign_structure.act_1: Party operates as Concord assets. Includes the Path of the Book — Dreknar is introduced through the Concord, and the party travels south through Slynn's Grey (deliver the Book to clergy for 'safeguarding') and onward to Havenford (deliver a locked sarcophagus for 'destruction'). Also involves Witherwood Forest. Party unknowingly delivers a corrupting artifact to an innocent community and transports a demon to the world's largest port city for release.
campaign_structure.act_2: Gloomwood Forest, east of Havenford. Treant encounters presented as a monster/nature problem. Clearing the treants opens the forest for Fastingbreak to harvest lumber for siege engine construction. The war machine becomes material reality.
campaign_structure.later: Magnus' group encounters the corrupted settlement at Slynn's Grey — clergy transformed into cultists from proximity to the Book. They stop an immediate threat and take possession of the artifact, never learning the full history of how it arrived there.

nations.name: Fastingbreak
nations.description: A once-powerful kingdom founded over a thousand years ago, whose early rulers united feuding tribes under the Crown. Fastingbreak flourished for centuries on fertile lands and dominance over Gulf of Everroth trade routes. Its inability to modernize has left it economically stagnant and dependent on Elkenquay for refined metals and magical components — a humiliating position for a proud kingdom with deep military heritage.
nations.history: Fastingbreak's southern border was contested for generations by Orcish tribes from the Iron Flats, leading to centuries of bloody conflict. The kingdom eventually secured the region, but the campaigns drained its resources and left lasting economic scars. This military heritage — and its cost — shapes the kingdom's identity and its current resentments.
nations.current_situation: King Althar Renwald IV, who inherited a kingdom already in decline, is being slowly tempted by the wizard Dreknar with promises of Zarathrax artifacts and restored military power. The Concord is manufacturing provocations against Elkenquay to push the kingdom toward war. Frustration among the populace grows as leadership clings to tradition.
nations.political_structure: Hereditary monarchy with a council of nobles advising the king. Power heavily centralized in the royal family.
nations.relationship_with_elkenquay: Deteriorating. Elkenquay has leveraged its trade dominance by increasing prices on refined metals and magical components. King Althar, under noble pressure and economic strain, increasingly sees war as the only means to reclaim lost prestige and secure the Umberstor Mountain resources.

nations.name: Elkenquay
nations.description: A flourishing republic of roughly 700 years, founded when disparate coastal villages united against pirate incursions in the Gulf of Everroth. Wealth from ports funded sophisticated mining in the Umberstor Mountains. About 300 years ago, merchants, miners, and shipbuilders led a peaceful revolution against the noble class, establishing the Senate. Today Elkenquay is renowned for innovation, inclusivity, and prosperity — and is Fastingbreak's primary economic and political rival.
nations.political_structure: Representative republic. Senate composed of elected officials serving fixed terms. Prime Minister elected from Senate ranks.
nations.current_situation: Genuinely not responsible for the provocations the Concord is manufacturing and attributing to them. However, they are economically pressuring Fastingbreak — raising prices and leveraging trade dominance — which gives the Concord real, legitimate grievances to weaponize. Prime Minister Trenwyth prefers diplomacy but is quietly readying defenses.
nations.key_assets: Arcane Transit Nexus (magically enhanced transportation network). Umberstor Mountains mining (iron, gold, gemstones, mithral, adamantine, Deeplight Crystals). Witherwood Forest (three ley lines, 15% of area magically active — key lumber resource). Control of major Gulf of Everroth shipping lanes.
nations.imports: Exotic spices and textiles through Havenford. Arcane components from Myrial.
nations.exports: Refined metals and enchanted tools globally. Lumber from Witherwood Forest to Havenford shipyards.
nations.gm_notes: Elkenquay is innocent of the specific provocations being manufactured. But they are not blameless in the broader geopolitical pressure — their trade leverage is real and is fueling genuine resentment. This nuance is important: the Concord's grievances are not entirely fabricated, just weaponized and amplified.

factions.name: The Concord
factions.description: A covert nationalist organization operating within Fastingbreak. Not officially part of the government, though it includes influential members from military, intelligence, trade, and political circles. Its members genuinely believe they are protecting their homeland — that Elkenquay is slowly strangling Fastingbreak and that passive waiting will lead to collapse.
factions.core_belief: Elkenquay is strangling Fastingbreak economically and politically. Conflict is not only inevitable but necessary for survival. The crown has been too slow to act.
factions.methods: Increase anti-Elkenquay sentiment. Manufacture or intensify border tensions. Create incidents that can be blamed on Elkenquay. Prepare Fastingbreak psychologically and politically for war. Operates in cells — most members only see their piece of any operation. Compartmentalized by design.
factions.tone: Not cartoon villains. A network of capable patriots who slowly convinced themselves that manipulation was necessary to save their country. Many members are frightened officials, opportunists, nationalists, and people who convinced themselves escalation was the only path.
factions.internal_secret: Sir Percival Malden is secretly funding a machinist inside Fastingbreak who creates the monstrosities. This is unknown to every other Concord member. The gem in the crate alongside the Book of the Damned was destined for Percival as a payment and power source for this research — a mission within the mission that no one else knows about.
factions.relationship_with_dreknar: The Concord views Dreknar as potentially useful for strengthening Fastingbreak militarily but fundamentally untrustworthy. They arranged for the kobolds to steal the crate specifically to intercept the Book before it could reach him — even they recognize giving him the Book would be dangerous. Despite this, they facilitate an introduction to the party because his resources are useful.

factions.name: The Order of Shadow and Flame
factions.description: A powerful and secretive organization serving Zarathrax's interests on the material plane. Fanatical worshippers who believe in the purity of the Shadowflame and seek to bring Zarathrax into the material realm. They believe the fusion of shadow and fire is the key to ultimate enlightenment and that Zarathrax is the one true sovereign of both worlds.
factions.methods: Infiltrates governments, cults, and organizations worldwide. Operates entirely in secret. Members are bound by pacts — power in exchange for service, often requiring sacrifice of something precious: a soul, a loved one, one's sanity.
factions.ultimate_goal: Summon Zarathrax fully into the material plane.
factions.connection_to_campaign: Dreknar is aligned with or a member of the Order. His guidance of the party — delivering the Book to Slynn's Grey and the sarcophagus to Havenford — serves the Order's broader agenda. The corruption of Slynn's Grey and the demon release at Havenford are Order operations conducted through unwitting proxies.

entities.name: Zarathrax
entities.title: The Shadowflame Lord of the Seventh Circle of Hell
entities.status: confined to the 7th Circle — not yet on the material plane
entities.description: An ancient being whose origins stretch back thousands of years, known in full only to a few souls locked by fate. Born under mysterious circumstances, with one or more Sylvan Queens of the Feywild present at his birth — possibly as part of a celestial alignment. He grew beyond the Feywild's natural boundaries, driven by an insatiable desire for dominance. He eventually forged his own path into Hell, where he made his defining discovery: the theft of shadow from the fabric of reality itself, fusing it with fire to create Shadowflame.
entities.power: The Shadowflame — a fusion of fire's consuming hunger and shadow's insidious nature. Paradoxical, volatile, uniquely his. The 7th Circle of Hell, once a place of fiery torment, was forever altered by his actions into a realm of dangerous beauty and terrifying contradictions.

army.shadow_beasts: Lower-rank foot soldiers. Tar-like, constantly shifting flesh, razor-sharp teeth capable of tearing through armor. Move on four legs, propelled by infernal flame and shadow magic. Swift and relentless.
army.greater_shadowflame_demons: Higher-ranking servants. Larger, stronger, more intelligent. Can wield fire or shadow independently — some summon infernos at will, others cloak themselves in shadow.
army.elite_winged_warriors: True captains of the Shadowflame army. Wings of living shadowflame. Their presence heralds destruction.

entities.history: Stole the essence of shadow from reality, shattering the balance between light, shadow, and flame. Seized the 7th Circle. The identity of the former lord of the circle is unknown — possibly slain, possibly banished, possibly never existed. Destroyed the city of Caldrath when its ruler broke a pact with him.
entities.caldrath: Once a great city. Its ruler believed he could wield shadow power without Zarathrax's consent and broke their pact. Zarathrax obliterated the city in moments. The ruins are now a cautionary legend whispered by those who know the name.
entities.feywild_connection: Sylvan Queens were present at his birth. His power has roots in the Feywild's untamed forces. The full significance of this connection is unknown and may become relevant later.
entities.relevance_to_campaign: Dreknar's true goal is to raise Zarathrax fully into the material plane. The Book of the Damned and the sarcophagus delivery to Havenford are both pieces of this plan. The Order of Shadow and Flame facilitates this on the material plane.

npcs.name: Princess Aurelia
npcs.description: A PC. Princess of Sarynthia. Eric Huwithakay was Sarynthia's intelligence contact embedded in Hollowgate — Aurelia knows this, and his correspondence has gone silent. She has been waiting for news that hasn't come. When the party finds Eric's body in the kobold tunnels, she is the one who understands what it means: not just a death, but a failed mission that no one back home knows about yet.
npcs.gm_notes: Aurelia's connection to Eric gives her personal stakes in the Hollowgate investigation beyond the rest of the party. Her Sarynthian background may allow her to automatically recognize Eric's signet stamp and the Elkenquay markings on the dying human. Her royal status and intelligence background should inform how she reads political situations as the campaign develops.

npcs.name: Robyn
npcs.description: A kobold scout and outcast from the cavern band near Hollowgate. Smaller than other kobolds, with softer coloration, a drooping uneven feather plume, and one badly-dyed feather. He was coerced into participating in the convoy raids — threatened with the loss of his tail feathers if he refused. He does not want to hurt anyone.
npcs.personality: Nervous, stammering, apologetic. Speaks quickly when frightened, talks over himself, flinches at loud sounds. Slouches, avoids eye contact, tail constantly twitching. Failed to become 'properly kobold' by his tribe's standards and is picked on for it.
npcs.what_robyn_knows: The kobolds were told to retrieve a specific crate — 'Boss said special. Not food crate.' A human hired them. He can lead the party toward the kobold caverns if they make him feel safe enough to do so.
npcs.key_quote: "I-I didn't wanna come with 'em... They said if I didn't, they'd... they'd tear off my tail feathers... I-I just wanted to stay in the nest..."
npcs.gm_notes: Robyn should feel oddly sympathetic — not heroic, just not built for kobold life. Responds much better to patience and kindness than intimidation. If the party treats him well, he becomes a useful recurring contact for kobold movements and tunnel layouts.

npcs.name: Krobb
npcs.description: The kobold guard stationed at the entrance to the cavern tunnels. Asleep when the party arrives. Slightly soft around the middle, wearing a helmet too large for his head. A sign on the wall behind him reads GUARD DUTY = VERY IMPORTANT! (PROBABLY). Krobb designed the tunnel trap system himself, drew a meticulous map, and is absolutely certain no one could ever get through without it. He was asleep when the party arrived and the pit trap was visible from the doorway.
npcs.personality: Absolute self-assurance about his trap designs, zero actual bravery. He believes his system is the finest in the tunnels, possibly the world. When genuinely threatened, this illusion evaporates instantly. His spear is somewhere. He will look for it. He will not find it in time.
npcs.what_krobb_knows: All trap locations, jail area layout, that the cavehopper is behind the rocks, that the two guards at the jail hate their assignment, and that the chieftain has the crate. He does not know what the crate contains.
npcs.cooperation: Immediate and total once threatened. Will describe every trap in loving detail, warn about the pit, confirm the cheese is not free, and describe the cavehopper's location in a hushed voice.
key_quotes: "Traps are... very good traps. Cannot pass without map. I make them. Very hard. Very clever. You would not get past."
"Wait — where is spear? I had spear. Spear was HERE."
npcs.trap_map: Hand-drawn on the back of a food manifest in charcoal. Misspelled throughout. Annotated with strong opinions about each trap's quality. At the bottom in larger letters: IF YOU READ THIS, YOU'RE PROBABLY DEAD. –KROBB
npcs.gm_notes: Player handout exists: krobb's trap map. Krobb is comic relief with mechanical function — his cooperation gives the party real tactical advantage. If he survived, he could be a recurring minor character who respects anyone clever enough to get through his work.

npcs.name: The Chieftain
npcs.description: The kobold chieftain of the cavern band — self-declared king. Unexpectedly charismatic, with a deep, rich, theatrical voice. Speaks like he believes he is a great king, because by his own reckoning he is. His royal regalia consists of molted feathers, broken necklaces, polished trash, and fake gemstones arranged with absolute sincerity. Simultaneously ridiculous and oddly compelling.
npcs.personality: Deeply proud. Theatrical. Savors his own words. Genuinely offended that the crate contained only a book — considers it a personal insult from the dying human who hired him. Not a villain; he just wanted to show off his pet.
npcs.role_in_story: Was hired by the dying human (a planted Concord false flag agent) to retrieve the specific crate. He knew the job, not the conspiracy — and the kobolds took the extra wagons because it seemed fair. His boss encounter is presenting the cavehopper as his greatest treasure. He wants admiration, not violence.
npcs.after_the_fight: Annoyed at the situation rather than the party. Waves them out through a back tunnel with evident impatience. The kobolds who follow are herding, not hunting — rocks and spears thrown more out of embarrassment than aggression.
key_quotes: "Little man hired us. Wanted shiny box. We took extra wagons too. Seemed fair."
npcs.gm_notes: Comedy with weight. His absolute sincerity makes him memorable. He is not present at the entry post during the Krobb scene — he stays in the den. Run him as genuinely uncertain whether the cavehopper fight is a problem or entertainment.

npcs.name: The Dying Human
npcs.status: dying — condition unknown after party encounter
npcs.description: A human found mauled and barely alive in the kobold chieftain's den. He wears colors associated with Elkenquay. Most parties will not recognize this immediately. He was the one who hired the kobolds and assigned them the crate retrieval — and the cavehopper, the chieftain's pet, turned on him afterward.
npcs.what_he_reveals: If stabilized, can provide fragments before losing consciousness: that he hired the kobolds specifically to retrieve the crate, and that the crate was supposed to go somewhere specific. He cannot or will not say more.
npcs.items: Elkenquay coinage. Partially burned correspondence. Coded ledger fragments. Tariff records. Convoy route maps. Seal fragments. References to 'adjusted taxation' and 'diverted cargo' — the same patterns Eric Huwithakay was tracking.
npcs.true_nature: His Elkenquay markings are the Concord's planted false flag — designed to make the party believe Elkenquay agents orchestrated the convoy theft. A DC 14 check identifies the Elkenquay association. Characters with noble, military, merchant, diplomatic, or espionage backgrounds may recognize them automatically.
npcs.gm_notes: This NPC is the anti-Elkenquay narrative made physical. His documents and appearance are manufactured evidence. He is the moment the party first begins to believe Elkenquay is the enemy — which is exactly what the Concord intended.

npcs.name: Eric Huwithakay
npcs.party_knowledge: known — personal contact, found dead in the tunnels
npcs.description: Not merely a contact — a Sarynthian intelligence operative embedded in Hollowgate, tracking unusual tariffs, altered manifests, and missing cargo. He got close enough to the truth that he was killed. The party finds his body in the kobold tunnels while escaping — already dead, satchel still on him. He never filed his final report.
npcs.what_is_found: A bronze signet stamp — Sarynthian, though most party members won't recognize it immediately. A hand-drawn partial tunnel map, more detailed than Krobb's — he had been here before. A final note in tight, careful handwriting: 'Kobolds. Someone is paying them.' That is all he had time to write.
npcs.cause_of_death: Not immediately clear. Not kobold weapons. The party may or may not connect this to the cavehopper on their own.
npcs.connection_to_aurelia: Princess Aurelia (a PC) knows Eric was Sarynthia's contact in Hollowgate. His correspondence stopped. She has been waiting for news that hasn't come. The party now carries it.
npcs.role_in_story: His death is the personal hook that pulls the party into the Hollowgate investigation. Finding him in the tunnels is the moment the convoy problem stops being a local nuisance and becomes something larger. Tomas Vale may have known him by name through customs work — a thread the party could pull.
npcs.gm_notes: Eric never knew the full scope of what he was uncovering. His death should feel like the cost of asking the right questions in a place where the wrong people were watching. The moment the party finds him should land with weight.

npcs.name: Cael Darro
npcs.party_knowledge: met — role unknown until recruitment pitch
npcs.cover: Traveling gambler and fast-talker. Charming, unremarkable at first glance.
npcs.reality: Low-level Concord operative. The party's recruiter. Knows Chaelynn and receives his assessment of the party through her.
npcs.description: A charming, fast-talking gambler with rapier wit and an actual rapier on his hip. Approximately two levels senior to the party. Has been embedded near or with the Moonlit Mirage Carnival, appearing to the party as a fellow traveler — someone who turns up at card games, buys rounds, and always seems to know what happened yesterday.
npcs.personality: Genuinely likeable. The kind of person you enjoy being around before you realize how carefully he's been watching you. Loses gracefully at cards, wins suspiciously. Never seems to be paying close attention but always knows more than he should. Believes in the Concord's mission at some level — this is not purely mercenary for him.
npcs.role_in_story: After the Concord extracts the party from the Dunmore monstrosity's aftermath, Cael appears — familiar face, friendly tone, already knowing things he shouldn't. He makes the recruitment pitch. His connection to Chaelynn gives him implicit credibility with the party.
npcs.gm_notes: Cael should be someone the players genuinely enjoy. He is not lying about everything — he believes the Concord is trying to save Fastingbreak. His charm is real. His concern for the party may even be real. That makes the eventual revelation of what the Concord has been doing harder to process.

npcs.name: Chaelynn
npcs.cover: Carnival worker with the Moonlit Mirage Carnival
npcs.reality: Concord operative. Observer and informant. Reports on the party to Cael Darro.
npcs.description: A long-lost contact of the party who is now working with the Moonlit Mirage Carnival — and also a Concord operative. The party knew her before the campaign; the specific nature of their history is established with the players. She has been with the carnival throughout the Prologue, watching the party handle both the Hollowgate aftermath and the Moonlit Mirage encounter.
npcs.role_in_story: Chaelynn is the chain of trust that makes the Concord recruitment feel organic. Her familiarity with the party — and theirs with her — means that when Cael approaches them with her implicit endorsement, the party listens. She is the bridge between their old life and the world the Concord is drawing them into.
npcs.gm_notes: Her history with the party should be established collaboratively before or early in the campaign. She may genuinely care for the party while also believing in the Concord's mission. That complexity is worth preserving.

npcs.name: Mirelle Voss
npcs.cover: Secretary to a noble household in Fastingbreak
npcs.reality: Concord operative. Identification and assessment specialist. Excellent at manipulation and social engineering. Identifies useful outsiders for the Concord.
npcs.description: A sharp, controlled woman who appears in social contexts — court-adjacent gatherings, noble household events — and observes far more than she reveals. She has access to political gossip and private correspondence.
npcs.personality: Controlled, charming, always reading the room. Expert at making people feel seen and understood without revealing anything about herself.
npcs.role_in_story: Likely the first Concord member to flag the party as potentially useful after the convoy recovery. She assesses and reports upward through the cell structure. She rarely makes direct contact herself.
npcs.gm_notes: Mirelle should seem entirely unremarkable if the party encounters her socially — pleasant, professional, forgettable. The retroactive realization of when she was watching them should be unsettling.

npcs.name: Garrick Thorne
npcs.reality: Key coordinator of staged disruptions. Tracks caravan movement and supply shortages. Coordinated the kobold raid on the convoy near Hollowgate.
npcs.description: A thoroughly unremarkable bureaucrat — exactly the kind of person no one looks twice at. Publicly boring. Privately extremely dangerous. Precise, organized, patient.
npcs.gm_notes: Garrick processed the logistics of the kobold raid. He likely does not know who ordered it at the highest level — he received instructions through the cell structure. If the party ever identifies him, his ordinariness should be disturbing.

npcs.name: Selene Marrow
npcs.cover: Assistant to a senior military advisor
npcs.reality: Has access to border readiness information and troop reports. Feeds strategic intelligence into Concord planning.
npcs.description: Calm, intelligent, persuasive. A true believer — she genuinely thinks conflict with Elkenquay is unavoidable and that the Concord is simply preparing Fastingbreak for what is coming regardless.
npcs.personality: The most ideologically committed of the field operatives. Not reckless — her convictions are measured and argued carefully. Which makes her more dangerous, not less.

npcs.name: Tomas Vale
npcs.cover: Clerk, Office of Customs and Revenue
npcs.reality: Economic warfare specialist. Understands how tariff and trade pressure affects border towns and manufactures economic grievances.
npcs.description: Detail-oriented, numbers-focused. Believes in the structural economic argument for conflict more than the nationalist one. To him, this is arithmetic: Fastingbreak's dependency on Elkenquay is unsustainable, and the only exit is force.
npcs.gm_notes: May have known Eric Huwithakay by name through customs work. This is a potential thread if the party digs into Eric's death.

npcs.name: Brother Carrow
npcs.reality: Moves messages between Concord cells. Recruits sympathetic locals quietly. Uses religious service as cover for travel.
npcs.description: Gentle demeanor, soft voice, easy manner. Deeply ideological beneath all of it. He believes the Concord's work is not just justified but morally necessary — almost righteous.
npcs.gm_notes: Brother Carrow is the face of the Concord that does not look like a face at all. He recruits not through pressure but through long, patient conversations. He should seem like a genuinely good person — because in many ways he is — who has allowed ideology to override ethics.

npcs.name: Sir Percival Malden
npcs.title: Master of Whispers
npcs.public_role: Oversees intelligence and espionage for the crown of Fastingbreak. His network of informants, codekeepers, and covert agents monitors threats both within Fastingbreak and beyond its borders. Very few people know how extensive his reach truly is.
npcs.secret_role: Secretly funds the machinist who creates the monstrosities. The gem in the crate alongside the Book of the Damned was destined for him — a payment and power source for the machinist's ongoing work, smuggled within the Concord's operation without any other member's knowledge.
npcs.personality: Controlled, observant, difficult to read. Rarely speaks more than necessary. Trusts almost no one and assumes everyone hides something — including allies. Percival does not panic. He adjusts.
npcs.relationship_to_concord: Does not command the Concord. Allows certain operations to continue, selectively withholds investigations, and occasionally provides intelligence through intermediaries. Views the Concord as dangerous but potentially necessary. His machinist project is entirely separate — not even the Concord knows.
npcs.loyalty: Officially to King Althar. In reality, to the survival of Fastingbreak above all else. Given a choice between morality and stability, he will choose stability.
npcs.gm_notes: The deepest player in the room. Should never feel like the obvious villain — too controlled and too quiet for that. The party should find him merely unsettling, not monstrous. The reveal of his machinist project should come late — Act 2 at the earliest — and the retroactive understanding of the gem should land like a gut punch.

npcs.name: Baron Aldric Varenthorne
npcs.title: Lord of Greymarch
npcs.description: A staunch traditionalist governing the heavily fortified border region of Greymarch — Fastingbreak's most strategically important territory facing Elkenquay. His lands maintain strong defensive walls, house veteran soldiers, and endure constant economic pressure from Elkenquay's shifting trade policies.
npcs.personality: Harsh, nationalistic, direct, uncompromising. Believes weakness invites conquest. Sees dissent, hesitation, and dependency as existential threats to the kingdom.
npcs.relationship_to_concord: Ideologically aligned. Quietly protects sympathetic operatives, supports aggressive preparation. Does not know every operation. Would deny public involvement. Believes the Concord exists because the crown has been too slow to act.
npcs.loyalty: Entirely to the king, the monarchy, and Fastingbreak's sovereignty. His definition of protecting the kingdom is increasingly militant.

npcs.name: King Althar Renwald IV
npcs.title: King of Fastingbreak
npcs.description: Ascended the throne after his father's untimely death in a hunting accident. Inherited a kingdom already in decline. Focuses on preserving tradition and maintaining the nobility's favor, often at the expense of modernization.
npcs.personality: Admired for commitment to the kingdom's heritage. Criticized for being out of touch with his people's actual needs. Susceptible to promises of restored prestige and power.
npcs.vulnerability: Being slowly tempted by Dreknar with promises of Zarathrax artifacts and increased military power. Does not understand what he is being drawn toward. He wants to believe someone has answers — Dreknar gives him a story where Fastingbreak can be great again.
npcs.gm_notes: Althar is not evil. He is a man in over his head who wants to believe someone has answers. That makes him pitiable — and dangerous, because his susceptibility makes him a tool in other people's wars.

npcs.name: Lord Harren Draeven
npcs.title: Chancellor of the Exchequer
npcs.description: A seasoned bureaucrat who has managed Fastingbreak's finances for decades. Skilled, calculating, and criticized for favoring noble estates over public infrastructure. Values stability above modernization.
npcs.loyalty: To King Althar and the noble class that keeps him in power.

npcs.name: Lady Elyse Marondel
npcs.title: Minister of Trade and Commerce
npcs.description: A shrewd negotiator from Greengate who has maintained Fastingbreak's trade routes despite growing Elkenquay competition. Charismatic, ambitious, and one of the more reform-minded members of court.
npcs.loyalty: Split between personal ambition and a genuine desire to revitalize the kingdom's economy.

npcs.name: General Kaelor Thrask
npcs.title: Commander of the Royal Army
npcs.description: A veteran of the Orcish Wars. Stoic, disciplined, respected. His aggressive stance toward Elkenquay has raised concerns among more diplomatic court members. Sees war as the only path through Fastingbreak's decline.
npcs.loyalty: Fiercely to the king. His hawkish policies are sincere conviction, not opportunism.

npcs.name: Archmage Lenira Vostral
npcs.title: Royal Advisor on Magical Affairs
npcs.description: A powerful sorceress who has served the royal family for three generations. Openly frustrated by the court's reluctance to invest in infrastructural magic. Intellectual, sharp-tongued, and unafraid to challenge the king.
npcs.loyalty: Pragmatic. Supports the crown but often aligns with reformists. Not ideologically committed to war.

npcs.name: Dreknar
npcs.party_knowledge: introduced at end of prologue — trusted, true role unknown
npcs.public_face: A scholar-wizard with deep knowledge of dangerous artifacts, apparently concerned with containing threats. Claims he has been tracking the Book of the Damned and wants it removed from circulation and properly safeguarded. Appears reasonable, credible, and aligned with the party's goals.
npcs.true_agenda: Raise Zarathrax into the material plane. Every operation he runs through the party — delivering the Book to Slynn's Grey, transporting the sarcophagus to Havenford — is a piece of this plan.
npcs.description: A wizard living outside Fastingbreak, introduced to the party by the Concord at the end of the Prologue. Patient, measured, and knowledgeable. He never pushes too hard. He has a credible story for everything, and the Concord's endorsement gives him a legitimacy he has not earned.
npcs.relationship_to_king: Promises King Althar access to Zarathrax artifacts and increased military power. Tempting the king slowly toward a dependency he does not understand.
npcs.relationship_to_concord: The Concord views him as useful but untrustworthy. They arranged the kobold theft specifically to prevent the Book reaching him. Despite this, they facilitate his introduction to the party because his resources serve their anti-Elkenquay agenda.
npcs.the_sarcophagus_mission: In Act 1, Dreknar gives the party a locked sarcophagus containing a raised demon and tells them it must be transported to Havenford for destruction. The actual goal is release at Havenford — the largest port city in the world. The party are unwitting couriers.
npcs.gm_notes: Dreknar should feel entirely trustworthy for a long time. Calm, knowledgeable, never aggressive. The horror of his manipulation is that it is nearly invisible. By the time the party understands what they helped him do, they will already have done it.

npcs.name: Prime Minister Calla Trenwyth
npcs.title: Prime Minister of Elkenquay
npcs.description: Born into a modest mining family in Highhaven. Rose through the Miner's Guild advocating for workers' rights — safer practices, fair wages. Her ability to bridge Senate factions made her a natural choice for Prime Minister. Pragmatic, empathetic, cautious with Fastingbreak — prefers diplomacy, but is quietly readying defenses.
npcs.key_achievement: The Arcane Transit Nexus — a network of teleportation circles and magically powered carriages connecting Elkenquay's major cities and mining sites.
npcs.gm_notes: Trenwyth is entirely innocent of the provocations attributed to Elkenquay. If the party ever reaches her level, she represents the human cost of what they have unknowingly helped engineer.

npcs.name: The Machinist
npcs.description: A researcher and scientist based inside Fastingbreak, secretly funded by Sir Percival Malden. Brilliant and practically blind to morality — he genuinely believes in the science of building better creatures and pursues the question of what the limits of biological design are. He does not particularly care what his creations are used for.
npcs.role_in_story: Entirely offscreen during the Prologue. The monstrosities the party encounters are his work. The gem in the crate was destined for Percival as a power source for this research.
npcs.gm_notes: Name not yet established. To be developed in Act 1 or Act 2. When eventually introduced, he should be fascinating and disturbing — not evil in any conventional sense, simply unconcerned with the moral weight of his work.

locations.name: Hollowgate
locations.description: A small town in Fastingbreak where the campaign begins. Site of the missing convoy investigation and the Moonlit Mirage Carnival. Supply wagons have been disappearing nearby — hired kobolds, coordinated through the Concord, have been raiding them. Eric Huwithakay was killed here.
locations.significance: The party's entry point into the larger conspiracy. The recovered crate — containing the Book of the Damned and the gem — sets every subsequent event in motion.

locations.name: Dunmore
locations.description: A proper Fastingbreak city — old stone, proud heritage, approximately ten thousand people. Large enough to have printing infrastructure, a visible noble presence, and real political weight. The party arrives here traveling with the Moonlit Mirage Carnival.
locations.situation: On the party's arrival, the city is in uproar. Propaganda and inflammatory pamphlets — anti-Elkenquay — are everywhere. The mood is a tinderbox. Someone has been manufacturing the outrage through the city's printing infrastructure.
locations.arc_summary: The party investigates the propaganda printing operation. Before resolution, a monstrosity breaks loose — overpowered, multi-phase. The situation spirals. The Concord extracts the party. Cael Darro makes the recruitment pitch. The Prologue ends.
locations.gm_notes: The second gem artifact is found on the Dunmore monstrosity. The propaganda investigation should give the party a moment of genuine clarity — someone is manufacturing this — before the monstrosity disrupts everything and the Concord steps in. Full Dunmore plot details to be developed.

locations.name: Slynn's Grey
locations.description: An isolated swamp settlement approximately three weeks' journey south of Fastingbreak on the road to Havenford. Home to a group of clergy with a reputation for safeguarding dangerous artifacts — the reason Dreknar directs the party here.
locations.fate: The clergy are entirely well-intentioned. They begin studying the Book of the Damned. Proximity and study corrupt them gradually over months — they become cultists. This happens entirely offscreen. Magnus' group later encounters the settlement deep into ritual and stops the immediate threat, never learning how the Book arrived.
locations.gm_notes: The party will likely never know what happened to Slynn's Grey during the campaign — unless they return. They delivered the Book in good faith. The weight of it only lands if they discover the corruption later.

locations.name: Havenford
locations.type: major port city — largest port in the world
locations.description: The largest port city in the world. A neutral hub that virtually every nation depends on for trade. Elkenquay imports exotic spices and textiles through Havenford and exports Witherwood lumber to its shipyards. Its independence and global significance make it an unthinkable target.
locations.significance: Dreknar's target for the sarcophagus release. The party will deliver a raised demon here believing they are escorting it to destruction. Releasing the demon at Havenford would be catastrophic on a global scale.
locations.gm_notes: The specific nature of the demon and mechanics of the release to be developed in Act 1.

locations.name: Greymarch
locations.description: The heavily fortified border territory governed by Baron Aldric Varenthorne. Strong defensive walls, veteran soldiers, constant economic pressure from Elkenquay's trade policies. A critical defensive point and a hotbed of nationalist sentiment.
locations.significance: Strategically vital for any conflict with Elkenquay. Aldric's base of power and the ideological heart of the Concord's militant wing.

locations.name: Eldralore
locations.description: The political and cultural capital of Elkenquay. East of Witherwood Forest, southwest of Highhaven, north of Port Valor. A harmonious blend of tradition, governance, and artistry.
locations.key_features: The Silver Spire — white stone and silver inlay, houses the Senate Chambers, in session one month per season. The Grand Market Plaza. The Unity Gardens surrounding the Silver Spire. The Songstone Amphitheater — enchanted stones amplify sound, built into a natural hillside.

locations.name: Port Valor
locations.description: The republic's largest city and primary port. Gateway to the Gulf of Everroth. Key hub for commerce, diplomacy, and innovation.
locations.key_features: The Lighthouse of Unity — powered by Deeplight Crystals. The Grand Harbor with magically enhanced cranes and storage.

locations.name: Highhaven
locations.description: Nestled in the Umberstor Mountains. Elkenquay's mining backbone — iron, gold, gemstones, mithral, adamantine, and the rare Deeplight Crystals. Prime Minister Trenwyth's hometown. Where the Arcane Transit Nexus began.
locations.key_features: The Skyforge Citadel — governance, Mountain Guild Council, elite artisans, defensive stronghold. The Emberstone Market. Deeplight Veins — closely guarded luminescent crystals used in enchantments and arcane technology.

locations.name: Coastfall
locations.description: Elkenquay's center for magical research and development. Arcane Foundries, the Academy of Applied Magic, and a Port of Knowledge hosting arcane symposiums drawing intellectuals from distant lands.
locations.key_features: Arcane Foundries with skilled enchanters and artificers. Academy of Applied Magic — practical magical disciplines. Exports enchanted tools, navigation devices, protective wards.

locations.name: Witherwood Forest
locations.description: A key Elkenquay resource forest at the conjunction of three ley lines. Approximately 15% of the area is magically active due to this alignment. Elkenquay exports lumber from Witherwood to Havenford's shipyards. Also a likely location for finding the energy capacitor gems.
locations.significance: Strategic Elkenquay lumber and magical resource. Ley line nexus makes it significant beyond economics. The party has a mission here in Act 1. Possible Feywild connection given Zarathrax's origins — to be developed later.

locations.name: Gloomwood Forest
locations.description: Located east of Havenford. The site of the treant encounters in Act 2. Clearing the treants opens the forest for lumber harvesting — providing Fastingbreak with material for siege engine construction. The party will believe they are addressing a nature threat. They are enabling a war.

locations.name: The Iron Flats
locations.description: The southern territory from which Orcish tribes launched repeated incursions into Fastingbreak for centuries. Securing this region drained Fastingbreak's resources and left lasting economic scars — a major contributor to the kingdom's current decline and its proud military identity.

locations.name: Caldrath
locations.description: Once a great city whose ruler broke a pact with Zarathrax, believing he could wield shadow power without the Lord's consent. Zarathrax obliterated it in moments — turned to ash, landscape scorched to wasteland. The ruins are now a cautionary legend whispered by those who know the name.

artifacts.name: The Book of the Damned
artifacts.status: party possession (Prologue) → delivered to Slynn's Grey (Act 1)
artifacts.description: A dangerous artifact that was being transported to Dreknar before the Concord arranged a kobold raid to intercept it. Even the Concord recognized that allowing it to reach Dreknar would be catastrophic. The party recovers it from the kobolds without knowing what it is.
artifacts.properties: Does not immediately corrupt. Instead slowly influences culture and deepens existing fanaticism through proximity and study over time. Those who attempt to study it are gradually transformed into something unrecognizable.
artifacts.journey: Recovered from kobolds at Hollowgate → held through Moonlit Mirage and Dunmore → delivered to Slynn's Grey clergy under Dreknar's guidance in Act 1 → corrupts the clergy offscreen → Magnus' group encounters the fallout later.
artifacts.gm_notes: The Book is a long-form weapon. Its danger is insidious and slow. The party's decision to deliver it to Slynn's Grey is entirely reasonable given what they know — which is exactly the point.

artifacts.name: The Gem (Energy Capacitor)
artifacts.status: multiple exist — one in crate (Prologue), others looted from monstrosities, more in Witherwood Forest (Act 1)
artifacts.description: Small gemstones that function as energy capacitors, amplifying and buffing vile creatures — particularly the monstrosities created by the machinist. The party first encounters them as loot from defeated monstrosities during the Prologue, without understanding their significance.
artifacts.significance: The gem in the crate alongside the Book was destined for Sir Percival Malden — smuggled within the Concord's operation without any other member's knowledge. It was a payment and power source for the machinist's research. Additional gems found in Witherwood Forest and on defeated monstrosities throughout the campaign.
artifacts.gm_notes: The gems are the physical thread connecting the monstrosity subplot to Percival's secret. When the party understands what the gems do and where they originate, the full picture of Percival's betrayal of even the Concord becomes clear. The party has been carrying this evidence without knowing what it means.

artifacts.name: The Locked Sarcophagus
artifacts.status: Dreknar's possession → given to party in Act 1
artifacts.description: A locked sarcophagus containing a raised demon — not a monstrosity. Different taxonomy, different origin, different threat level entirely. Dreknar presents it to the party in Act 1 claiming it must be transported to Havenford for destruction.
artifacts.true_purpose: Release at Havenford — the largest port city in the world. The party are unwitting babysitters. The release would be catastrophic globally.
artifacts.gm_notes: The specific nature of the demon and the mechanics of the release to be established in Act 1 development. The sarcophagus should feel ominous but containable — heavy with implied threat rather than visible danger.

arcs.title: Missing Convoys
arcs.summary: Supply wagons have been disappearing near Hollowgate. Eric Huwithakay, a contact of one of the party members, was investigating and has been killed. The party takes over the investigation, tracks the missing goods to kobolds in caverns nearby, and recovers a sealed crate among the stolen goods.
arcs.what_actually_happened: The Concord, through Garrick Thorne, hired the kobolds to raid the caravans — creating shortages and instability, and intercepting one specific sealed crate (the Book of the Damned + the gem) before it could reach Dreknar. Eric Huwithakay was killed because he was getting too close to the truth.
arcs.what_party_knows: Kobolds were stealing the goods. Eric is dead. The crate is sealed and clearly significant. Evidence near the raid scene suggests Elkenquay involvement.
outcomes: Party recovers grain, goods, and sealed crate intact
Planted or circumstantial evidence reinforces anti-Elkenquay narrative
Party begins to believe Elkenquay is destabilizing the region
arcs.hooks_forward: The crate becomes central in Act 1. The anti-Elkenquay narrative the party now partly believes is the Concord's manufactured justification for war.

arcs.title: Moonlit Mirage Carnival
arcs.summary: The Moonlit Mirage Carnival arrives in Hollowgate. The party encounters Chaelynn, a long-lost contact now working with the carnival. A Pallor Brood attack disrupts the carnival — monstrosities from the machinist's work.
arcs.what_actually_happened: The monstrosities are the machinist's creations, funded secretly by Percival. The gems dropped by the creatures are the same type as the gem in the crate. Chaelynn has been observing the party throughout and will report to Cael Darro.
arcs.what_party_knows: Strange creatures attacked the carnival and were defeated. Chaelynn is here — a familiar and trusted face. Gems were found on the creatures (significance unclear).
outcomes: Party survives the Pallor Brood encounter
Chaelynn reestablished as a trusted party contact
Concord's assessment of the party sharpens — Chaelynn's report goes to Cael
arcs.hooks_forward: Chaelynn's report sets the Dunmore recruitment in motion. The gems will become significant once the party understands their connection to the crate.

arcs.title: Dunmore
arcs.summary: The party travels to Dunmore with the carnival. The city is in uproar — inflammatory pamphlets everywhere, anti-Elkenquay sentiment at a boiling point. The party investigates who is printing and distributing the material. Before the investigation resolves, a monstrosity breaks loose — overpowered, multi-phase. The situation spirals. The Concord extracts the party. Cael Darro makes his pitch. The Prologue ends with the party recruited.
arcs.what_actually_happened: The propaganda is a Concord operation. The monstrosity is another machinist creation. The Concord's rescue is deliberate — timed to create obligation and trust. The party has been manipulated into position.
arcs.what_party_knows: Someone is manufacturing the anti-Elkenquay sentiment. A monstrosity attacked the city. The Concord saved them and appears to be the only organized group that knows what is actually happening.
outcomes: Second gem artifact recovered from the Dunmore monstrosity
Concord extracts the party from the aftermath
Cael Darro reveals the Concord and makes the recruitment pitch
arcs.hooks_forward: Party now works for the Concord. Dreknar will be introduced. The Book's journey south begins. Act 1 opens.
